name: Chicken Kiev (Nfwf89a)
recipeInstructions:
1. Flatten the chicken breasts to a thickness of about 1/4 inch by placing them between pieces of wax paper and pounding them lightly with a rolling pin or flat-sided kitchen mallet. Lay out the breasts, boned side up (the uneven side).
2. Salt and pepper each breast half to taste.
3. Place a small amount of butter on each breast half.
4. Roll up the butter in the breast meat as you would wrap a package, tucking in the ends at the beginning.
5. Dip the rolled breasts first in flour, then in beaten egg, and finally in bread crumbs.
6. Chill in the refrigerator for about 3 hours or more.
7. Heat oil to 365°F (182°C) in a saucepan or deep skillet. The oil should be deep enough to cover the breasts.
8. Deep fry the breasts for 4-5 minutes, uncrowded or until golden brown. Drain on paper towels and serve immediately. If frying in batches, keep others warm in an oven. I like these served with warm Hollandaise sauce and a very slight sprinkle of dill for color. Enjoy.
